title: On the Deluge.

source.title: ANF06. Fathers of the Third Century: Gregory Thaumaturgus, Dionysius the Great, Julius Africanus, Anatolius, and Minor Writers, Methodius, Arnobius

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: God decreed to destroy the whole race of the living by a flood, having threatened that men should not survive beyond 120 years.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: Nor let it be deemed a matter of difficulty, because some lived afterwards a longer period than that.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: For the space of time meant was 100 years up to the flood in the case of the sinners of that time; for they were 20 years old.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: God instructed Noe, who pleased him on account of his righteousness, to prepare an ark; and when it was finished, there entered it Noe himself and his sons, his wife and his daughters-in-law, and firstlings of every living creature, with a view to the duration of the race.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And Noe was 600 years old when the flood came on.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And when the water abated, the ark settled on the mountains of Ararat, which we know to be in Parthia; but some say that they are at Celænæ of Phrygia, and I have seen both places.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And the flood prevailed for a year, and then the earth became dry.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And they came out of the ark in pairs, as may be found, and not in the manner in which they had entered, viz., distinguished according to their species, and were blessed by God.

verses.book: The Extant Fragments of the Five Books of the Chronography of Julius Africanus.
verses.text: And each of these things indicates something useful to us.
